t_ch: <size=36>「缭乱」！「缭乱」！「缭乱」！</size>
t_en: <size=36>Dazzling Ninja! Dazzling Ninja! Dazzling Ninja!</size>

t_ch: 看来诸音忍经历此次修行，都大有长进啊。
t_en: Seems like all the sound ninjas have made significant progress through this training.

t_ch: 如此甚好。那么，便由在下宣布——「{TEXTJOIN#120}」的最终作战，于此时正式开启！
t_en: Very well. Then, I shall announce — the final show of {TEXTJOIN#120} will begin now!

t_ch: 请欣赏我等带来的，至为精彩的演出吧！
t_en: Please enjoy the spectacular performance we bring to you!

t_ch: …呼…呼。
t_en: ...Huff ...Huff.

t_ch: 音律•狩猎——开始！
t_en: Sound Hunt — begin!

t_ch: ——开始！
t_en: ...Begin!

t_ch: 这就是我们「{TEXTJOIN#120}」的乐队精神啊，那些学院代表有没有这种精神啊？
t_en: This is the spirit of {TEXTJOIN#120}. Do those representatives of the academy have this spirit?

t_ch: 充分感受到了吗，何为「{TEXTJOIN#120}」的音乐魅力——
t_en: Can you fully feel the musical allure of {TEXTJOIN#120}!!!

t_ch: 只要乱破忍首一声令下，我们立刻拥护她为新的谐乐学院代表！
t_en: As long as Head Ninja Rappa gives the order, we will immediately support her as the new representative of Charmony Academy!

t_ch: 首先我不是学校的支持者，我看到这场演出的时候已经毕业很久了。但不得不说，老师对学生的教导真的很重要，其实我觉得演奏正常音乐也没有什么不好的。
t_en: Firstly, I am not a supporter of the school. I already graduated long before I saw this performance. But I must say that a teacher's instruction is of utmost importance for their students. In fact, I believe that there's no harm in playing conventional music.

t_ch: 不得不讲，老师的想法还是比学生要来得正确。但我遇到这种事也不会强求，只是觉得，他们要是能多听听学院的意见就好了。
t_en: I must say, a teacher's views are often more accurate than students'. But I wouldn't insist on this. I only feel that they should be more open to listening to the opinions of the school.

t_ch: 你还真是只想着自己呢。
t_en: You're really only thinking for yourself.

t_ch: 没有没有。我就是随便说说，随便说说而已……
t_en: No no. It was just a passing remark, just a passing remark...

t_ch: （闭幕演出顺利完成，音乐派对也临近尾声了吧，莫名有点舍不得啊。）
t_en: (The closing act has been successfully completed, and the music party is nearing its end. There is an odd sense of reluctance to see it end.)

t_ch: 你沉浸在现场的喧闹气氛中，度过了一段难忘的时光。
t_en: You immerse yourself in the lively atmosphere of the event, spending an unforgettable time.

t_ch: 乱破忍首——
t_en: Head Ninja Rappa!

t_ch: 我打听到新乐队的消息了，他们也是临时组织起来的，还在手忙脚乱地为演出做准备。
t_en: I've heard some news about the new band. They were also assembled on short notice and are still scrambling to prepare for their performance.

t_ch: 要是好好交涉，把登台机会让给我们的概率很大！
t_en: If the negotiation goes well, there's a high chance that they'll give us the opportunity to perform!

t_ch: 筑梦学院不会干涉我们的行动，比较麻烦的…就是那两位学院代表。
t_en: Dreamweaver Academy won't interfere with our actions, but what's more troubling are... those two representatives of the academy.

t_ch: 感谢诸位倾力相助！如今主公归来，登台的时限又将至，我等「缭乱」不得不分兵作战，想办法将强敌逐个击破了。
t_en: Thank you, everyone, for all your help! Now that Sound Master has returned, and our time on stage is about to end, the Dazzling Ninja must split into groups and beat our rivals in the contest.

t_ch: ——键盘•忍徒，贝斯•忍徒，可否请二位继续研习音律•忍术，为后续作战做万全准备？
t_en: ...Ninja Initiate Keyboard, Ninja Initiate Bass — will you keep studying Sound Ninjutsu, and organize full preparations for subsequent contests?

t_ch: 没问题！要是找不到排练的地方，可以来我家的地下室，都是顶级设备！
t_en: No problem! If we can't find a place to practice, you can all use my house's basement — it's all top-notch equipment!

t_ch: 嗯。编曲方面来不及的话，我也可以帮忙。
t_en: Yes, if there's not enough time for composition, I can help out too.

t_ch: 此外，既然得知吉他•忍徒是被贼人所困，作为忍首，在下绝不能置之不理。
t_en: Also, now that we've learned Ninja Initiate Guitar has been imprisoned by scoundrels, I cannot stand idle as Head Ninja.

t_ch: 「缭乱」的任何成员都不可或缺。在下这就出发，誓要将吉他•忍徒从邪魔手中救出，重返最终演台！
t_en: No one member of the Dazzling Ninja will be left behind. I will set off now — I swear to rescue Ninja Initiate Guitar from the clutches of evil, and return to partake in the final stage!

t_ch: 还能赶得上演出吗？
t_en: Will you be able to make it back in time?

t_ch: 我的任务呢？
t_en: What about my task?

t_ch: 梦土的道路曲折且长，踏上此行不知何时能复返，然无畏的忍侠决意要前往，只因过往的同伴正身陷苦难。若是在下未能归还——
t_en: The path of dreams is meandering. I know not when I shall return on my journey, but this ninja hero shall forge forth undaunted, unwavering — if for nothing more than the fact that my companion has been entrapped in danger. If I do not return...

t_ch: 就要烦请主公为我等争取更多时限了。
t_en: Please buy more time for me, Sound Master.

t_ch: 乱破无言地扶住了帽檐，你能预感到激烈的忍者•战斗即将在某处一触即发。
t_en: Rappa silently grasps the brim of her hat — you can foresee an intense Ninja Battle about to occur in some unknown time and place.

t_ch: 在下以「缭乱忍侠」之名承诺——演台开幕之际，在下必定携吉他•忍徒共同登场。
t_en: I vow on my name as a Dazzling Ninja — When the curtain raises over the stage, I will surely step forth with Ninja Initiate Guitar by my side.

t_ch: 闯荡忍界至今，在下从未有过食言。请阁下尽管放心。
t_en: I have never reneged on my word in all my ninja travels. Worry not, my lord.

t_ch: 等等——不是！小矮个，我可从来没让你开除艾尔菲啊！
t_en: Hang on, wait! Shortie, I never told you to expel Arfey!

t_ch: 虽然她是被那个外校生带坏了，但不管变成什么样，她都是我最自豪的学生！
t_en: She may have fallen under the delinquent influence of exchange students, but no matter what, she's my most prized pupil!

t_ch: 没有我的允许，谁敢动谐乐学院的学生！来，试试看啊！
t_en: Who dares take liberties with the Charmony Academy's students without my approval! Come on, just try it!

t_ch: 骂谁矮呢？你也配说这句话？…让开！别耽误我教育问题学生，整顿校风！
t_en: Who're you calling short? You think yourself fit to say that? ...Get away! Don't hinder me from educating difficult students and upholding school ethics!

t_ch: ——哼哼。
t_en: ...Heh-heh.

t_ch: 感谢诸位为在下拖延时限！
t_en: Thank you all for helping me delay my deadline!

t_ch: 缭乱忍侠•AKA乱破！
t_en: The Dazzling Ninja a.k.a. Rappa!

t_ch: ——前来参见！
t_en: ...seeks audience!

t_ch: 临时登台还请行个方便——
t_en: Hope you don't mind my last-minute appearance on stage...

t_ch: （排练室里…怎么好像有说话的声音。）
t_en: (In the rehearsal room... I think I hear voices chatting.)

t_ch: （闭幕演出就快开始了，时间确实很紧张。）
t_en: (The closing show is about to start. Time is definitely running short.)

t_ch: （咦，乱破身边站着的是…芮克先生？）
t_en: (Huh? That person standing beside Rappa... Isn't that Mr. Reca?)

t_ch: （乱破已经出发了，现在就去舞台附近看看吧。）
t_en: (Rappa has already left. Let's take a look around the stage.)

t_ch: （契波和达凡绮都上台了，过去看看情况吧。）
t_en: (Czipo and Dafanchy have gone on stage. Let's head over and take a look.)

t_ch: （时间所剩无几，乱破还没有回来，或许…得想想别的办法了。）
t_en: (There's not much time left, and Rappa's still not back. Maybe... we have to think of some other idea.)

t_ch: 芮克先生离开后不久……
t_en: Not long after Mr. Reca leaves...

t_ch: 就这样，你们被赶出了排练室……
t_en: Just like that, you were all kicked out of the rehearsal room...

t_ch: 我是被那个坏家伙给骗了…都是财富学院的错！我…我糊涂啊！
t_en: I was fooled by that scoundrel... It's all the Fortune Academy's fault! I... I was misled!

t_ch: 每个人都有被看见的渴望。听着乐队同伴们精彩的演奏，你的心中油然生出了闪亮登场的冲动。
t_en: Everyone yearns to be seen. Listening to the band's splendid performance, an impulse to light up the stage blooms in your heart.

t_ch: （看我悄悄溜上舞台……）
t_en: (Let's quietly creep onto the stage...)

t_ch: 喂，你想干什么？！干扰舞台演出是吧，再乱来的话我就要举报你了！
t_en: Hey, what're you doing!? Trying to disrupt the show, are you? I'll report you if you do anything funny again!

t_ch: （看我悄悄溜上舞台……）
t_en: (Let's quietly creep onto the stage...)

t_ch: 狂热也得有个限度吧！演出现场不是法外之地，注意点！
t_en: There's to be a limit to your enthusiasm! The stage isn't a lawless place! Watch yourself!

t_ch: （看我悄悄溜上舞台……）
t_en: (Let's quietly creep onto the stage...)

t_ch: 还来？…实在是忍无可忍了，朋友们，我们集体把这个不守规矩的家伙给轰出去！
t_en: Again? ...Absolutely incorrigible. Friends, let's work together and toss this unruly trespasser out!

t_ch: （看我悄悄溜上舞台……）
t_en: (Let's quietly creep onto the stage...)

t_ch: 还未等你采取行动，几位身形壮硕的观众已然站在了你身边，无形的压力仿佛是堵高墙，将你与近在咫尺的舞台隔绝开来。
t_en: Before you can even make a move, several burly members of the audience are standing beside you, their imposing auras looming over you, and completely zoning you out of the stage that is inches away.

t_ch: 看来你的演艺梦想终究是要止步于此了。
t_en: Looks like your dream of performing is about to be put to an end here.

t_ch: 艾尔菲，你听我说，当时我是被那个坏家伙给骗了啊…都是财富学院的错！
t_en: Arfey, listen to me, I was lied to by that scoundrel... It was all Fortune Academy's fault!

t_ch: 他告诉我说，学校领导对乐队的表现很不满，要是继续演出，就禁止你在匹诺康尼进行任何音乐活动。我也是为了你的前途着想啊！
t_en: He told me that the school's higher-ups were very displeased with the band's performance, and they would ban you from all music events in Penacony if they continued playing. I was just thinking about your future!

t_ch: 这是我专门为你们乐队手抄的乐谱。就当是给你赔罪了。
t_en: Here's the music score I copied for you by hand. Take it as compensation.

t_ch: 这个，不需要了。
t_en: Um, I don't need it.

t_ch: 但事实上，财富学院的那位代表处处刁难乐队，不过是因为「晖长石号」上的几处店面是奥帝先生答应给他的生日礼物，却随飞艇一同被转赠给了星穹列车。
t_en: But honestly, the reason the Fortune Academy's representative has been haranguing us non-stop is only because Mr. Alfalfa promised to give him ownership of some stores aboard the Radiant Feldspar, which was instead gifted to the Astral Express, airship and all.

t_ch: 苜蓿草家系已经撤销他的学院代表职位，相信音乐派对的后续活动不会再受到干扰。
t_en: The Alfalfa Family has already rescinded his rep position in the academy. I trust any subsequent events in the music party will not be disrupted.

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yeah!

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yeah!

t_ch: 这就是和我作对的下场！
t_en: This is what you get for opposing me!

t_ch: 喔！这就是和我们音律忍者作对的下场！
t_en: Ho! This is what you get for opposing the sound ninja!

t_ch: 老奥帝的亲戚…是不是有点多？
t_en: Do you think... Old Oti has too many relatives?

t_ch: 与那位代表所说的情况恰好相反，「缭乱」在观众里的人气相当高，学校主办方收到了大量的粉丝来信，要求增加乐队的演出场次，并保留相关的场地布设。
t_en: The truth is the exact opposite of what that representative claimed — the Dazzling Ninja is a smash hit among the crowd. The school's organizer received loads of fan mail requesting encores of the band's performance and even keeping mementos from the venues.

t_ch: 匹诺康尼在此次活动期间的游客数量剧增，学生们展现出的自主精神和热情也打消了外界对秩序余波的担忧，为梦境在银河中重新赢得了赞誉。
t_en: Penacony's tourism has been on the rise during the event. The proactivity and passion of the students have also quelled the worries of the concerns other worlds had towards the remnants of Order, renewing the Dreamscape's repute among the galaxy.

t_ch: 因此，折纸大学决定——延长音乐派对的举办时间，属于学生们的舞台还会继续。
t_en: Thus, Paperfold University has decided to extend the music party's duration — the student's stage will remain open.

t_ch: 好耶！
t_en: Yeah!

t_ch: 永不结束的音乐节！
t_en: A music festival that never ends!

t_ch: 这样的话…即使成绩不好的同学，也能有自己的表现机会了。
t_en: If that's the case... even students with poor results will also have a chance to perform.

t_ch: 不过，乱破忍首在哪呢？演出结束后好像…就没看到她。
t_en: But where's Head Ninja Rappa? I... haven't seen her since the show ended.

t_ch: 乱破小姐先前曾拜托音乐派对的工作人员，把乐队使用的乐器都搬到排练室。现在过去的话，应该还能碰到她哦。
t_en: Miss Rappa asked a music party staff member to move the band's instruments to the rehearsal room. If you head there now, you might still be able to meet her.
Hmm...

t_ch: 你在思考什么呢？
t_en: What are you thinking about?

t_ch: 不会还要搞个大的吧？
t_en: You're not still trying to end with a bang, are you?

t_ch: 非也。在下只是突然有些伤感。
t_en: Nay. I am somewhat saddened, is all.

t_ch: 即便作战的盛况空前，诸位也将音律•忍法演绎得淋漓尽致，然…散场之时终至。
t_en: Even though the contest was unprecedentedly magnificent, every one of you played Sound Release ninjutsu with pitch-perfect brilliance. Alas... all shows must end.

t_ch: 「人世间，尘与风，去留无端，恍若梦中之梦。」
t_en: "All things in this world come and go with neither rhyme nor reason, as if in a dream of dreams."

t_ch: 喂喂，说好的要教我忍术呢。
t_en: Hey, you promised to teach me ninjutsu.

t_ch: 别把气氛闹得这么沉重，演出虽然结束了，但我们的乐队可没有解散啊。
t_en: Don't be such a Debbie Downer. The show may be over, but our band hasn't disbanded.

t_ch: 就算从学校毕业了，也还要一起演奏！
t_en: Even if we graduate, we still gotta jam together!

t_ch: 就是就是！
t_en: No, you won't!

t_ch: 啊？我都准备退休了。
t_en: Eh? I'm ready to retire though.

t_ch: 诸位都找到了各自的忍道，便无需在下继续代为统率了。
t_en: Everyone has found their way of the ninja. There is no more need for me to continue leading it.

t_ch: 「缭乱」并非是因缘•聚散的团体，更应是我等的信念传承。音律终会停止，但音律•忍者的狩猎不会结束。往后的道路，只能由诸位自己决定。
t_en: The Dazzling Ninja is not a group bound or scattered by fate — it is a legacy of our beliefs. The music will inevitably cease, but the sound ninja's hunt will never end. It is up to everyone to decide the path forward.

t_ch: 乱破小姐…你真的打算离开吗？
t_en: Miss Rappa... Are you really thinking of leaving?

t_ch: 在下参加此次音律祭典，原本便是受阁下所托，协助诸位忍徒完成试炼。
t_en: I originally participated in this music festival at your behest, to assist you all in your trials.

t_ch: 原来是知更鸟小姐的委托吗？
t_en: So it was a request from Miss Robin?

t_ch: 原来不是你在胡闹？
t_en: So you weren't just messing around?

t_ch: …是的，我发现了这次校庆中的部分校方人员在滥用职权，但碍于嘉宾的身份，没办法下场帮助这些学生。
t_en: ...No. I noticed that some school authorities were abusing their power during the anniversary celebration, but as a guest-of-honor, I could not directly help these students.

t_ch: 所以才会拜托乱破小姐担任乐队核心，让音乐派对顺利举办下去。
t_en: Which is why I asked Miss Rappa to become the band leader and facilitated the success of the music party.

t_ch: 如今，藏在暗处的恶徒已经伏诛，音忍的修行完满结束，在下也该启程寻找新的去处。
t_en: And now, the evildoers lurking in darkness have been subjugated, and the sound ninja's training is complete. My journey must begin anew.

t_ch: 折纸大学的音乐派对还会持续很长时间，没有你们的演出，整场活动都会失去很多乐趣吧，学生们也会觉得无聊的。
t_en: Paperfold University's music party will continue for a very long time. Without your performances, the entire event would probably lose its luster aplenty, and the students would be bored too.

t_ch: 歌舞•忍者无须担心，在下早已准备妥当，曾修行过的虚偃•忍术，正好在此时派上用场。
t_en: Songstress Ninja, you need not be concerned. I have long since made preparations — the Void Ninjutsu that I once learned will come in very handy for such moments.

t_ch: 在下的忍•幻影将始终立于演台之上，以至为响亮的音律向所有来者宣告——
t_en: My Ninja Mirage will evermore remain on the stage, to proclaim unto all comers with a resounding melodious sound...

t_ch: ——我等，即是「缭乱」！
t_en: ...We are the Dazzling Ninja!

t_ch: 故事完结，演员离场……
t_en: The story comes to a close, and the actors leave the set...

t_ch: 副导演，我们也该去寻找新的剧本了。
t_en: Assistant director, we should also go find a new script.

t_ch: …{NICKNAME}演员，你为我带来的演出很有趣，期待下个片场还能与你合作——
t_en: ...{NICKNAME}, you've given me a most interesting performance indeed. I look forward to working with you again in our next piece...

t_ch: CUT。
t_en: CUT.

t_ch: 咕咕！你回来了，这次的晚会真好看咕！
t_en: Hoot hoot! You're back! The evening show was amazing!

t_ch: 猫头鹰老师好！
t_en: Hello, Professor Owl!

t_ch: 咕咕，真有礼貌，就像镜子公主一样。
t_en: Hoot hoot, so polite, just like the Mirror Princess.

t_ch: 那当然！
t_en: Definitely!

t_ch: 咕咕，真是自信，就像钟表小子一样。
t_en: Hoot hoot, so confident, just like Clockie.

t_ch: 我觉得还有提升空间。
t_en: I think there's room for improvement.

t_ch: 咕咕，真是谦虚，就像左轮队长一样。
t_en: Hoot hoot, so humble, just like Captain Revolver.

t_ch: 小鸟们追逐梦想的身影，不管看多少次，都还是很感动咕！
t_en: Birds chasing the shadows of their dreams — always so touching, no matter how many times I watch it!

t_ch: 咕咕，让我给你讲个故事吧——关于这片折纸树林的故事。
t_en: Hoot hoot, let me tell you a story — about Paperfold Woods.

t_ch: 很久很久以前，老鸟收到了一封恩人的邀请函。于是老鸟只身来到了美梦小镇，帮助这里的镇民修建房屋。
t_en: Once upon a time, an old bird received an invitation from a benefactor. So that bird came to the Dreamville, to help its residents build houses.

t_ch: 在这里，我认识了一群非常厉害的朋友：一把能打垮一百条鳄鱼的手枪，一面能掏出各种知识的镜子，一只让恶人闻风丧胆的野狼，还有那块无所不能的小钟表……
t_en: It was here that I befriended a group of amazing people: A revolver that could defeat a hundred crocodiles, a mirror that brimmed with knowledge, a wild wolf that made villains freeze in fear, and that little clock that could do just about anything...

t_ch: 老鸟以为，这座小镇有了他们的保护，不会被世界上的任何东西破坏，永远不会。
t_en: The old bird thought that with their protection, this town would never be destroyed by anything in the world, for ever more.

t_ch: 说到这里，猫头鹰老师的声音突然变了，他的声音变得沧桑、厚重，还有一丝…哀伤。
t_en: At this point, Professor Owl's voice suddenly changes — it becomes raspy, dignified, with a sliver of... sorrow.

t_ch: ——但我错了，世界太大，永远太长。在这两个无穷的尺度组成的坐标轴中，再伟大的英雄也显得微不足道。
t_en: ...But I was wrong. The world is too vast and an eternity too long. Within the coordinates of these two fathomless dimensions, even the greatest heroes would appear insignificant.

t_ch: 于是，我选择建立这片树林，希望为小镇培养出一群厉害的折纸小鸟。当钟表小子和他的朋友们无法保护小镇时，这里的小鸟们团结一心，或许能抵御未来的风暴。
t_en: And so, I chose to erect this forest, hoping to raise a flock of strong origami birds. When Clockie and his friends could not protect the town, the birds here would be united in heart, perhaps able to weather any future storms.

t_ch: 即便不能…我也希望他们有能力在风暴过后的废墟中保护自己，有能力在一个荒芜的时代追逐自己的梦想。
t_en: Even if they could not... I still hoped that their abilities would allow them to protect themselves in the aftermath of the storm — the capability to pursue their own dreams in a barren era.

t_ch: 你的声音怎么变了……
t_en: Why has your voice changed...

t_ch: 你怎么不咕咕了……
t_en: Why aren't you hooting anymore...

t_ch: 你到底是谁……
t_en: Who exactly are you...

t_ch: 不好意思——咕咕咕！鸟上了年纪，就容易想起往事，老鸟太唠叨了吧！
t_en: Oh, my apologies — Hoot hoot! As we get on in age, we reminisce far too easily. This old bird is far too chatty!

t_ch: 新同学，你带来的演出很好看，让老鸟想起了当年的钟表小子！他也曾带着自己的伙伴们在晚会上起舞哦！
t_en: New student, your performance was very good — it made this old bird think back on Clockie in the younger days! He also once roped in his buddies to dance the evening away in the festival!

t_ch: 有这么多心怀梦想的折纸小鸟，美梦小镇的未来…一定会更好的。
t_en: So many origami birds bearing dreams — the future of the Dreamville... will surely be better.

t_ch: 咕咕咕，愿老鸟和钟表小子想出来的校训，能指引你们不惧前行——
t_en: Hoot hoot, may this old bird and Clockie's school mantra guide you all forward, steadfast and fearless...

t_ch: 「美梦多彩，理想自由」。
t_en: "Color your dreams, free your ideals."

t_ch: 来来。请选择你的苏乐达瓶，猜猜看，哪个瓶子里的汽水喷得最高？
t_en: Come, come. Pick your SoulGlad bottle and have a guess: Which soda will spray the highest?

t_ch: 答对有奖啊，奖品就是——之前风靡校园的「睡蕉小猴玩偶」！
t_en: You get a prize for the right answer! The prize is... a Slumbernana Monkey doll that once took the campus by storm!

t_ch: 喂，这些不会是你没卖完的存货吧，现在谁还粉这种傻傻的过气角色啊。
t_en: Hey, don't tell me these are left over from the stock you didn't manage to sell. Who'd stan such a dopey, outdated character?

t_ch: 我选第四个瓶子！是我的幸运数字！
t_en: I choose the fourth one! It's my lucky number!

t_ch: 在围观什么呢？
t_en: What are you all looking at?

t_ch: 让我看看！
t_en: Let me see!

t_ch: 这不是我们的经理嘛！来，你也玩玩。几个苏乐达瓶不知道怎么回事，总在喷汽水。
t_en: If it isn't our manager! Come play a round too. No idea what's up with these SoulGlad bottles, apart from the fact that they're spraying soda all the time.

t_ch: 猜猜，哪个瓶子里的苏乐达会喷得最高？没有提示，全靠运气！
t_en: Take a guess. Which bottle's SoulGlad will spray the highest? No hints, all luck!

t_ch: 这就是大学生的日常吗？
t_en: This is what college students do all day?

t_ch: 是啊，课程轻松，生活不愁，时间太多，乐子不够。哪里好玩去哪里。
t_en: Yeah, classes are easy, life is cushy, we've got too much time, and we can't have enough fun. We go where the entertainment is.

t_ch: 学分拿满了，答辩没压力，毕业后的方向也定了…嗨，就等乱破小姐带我回家了。
t_en: Earning full credits, no stress in appraisals, where to go after graduation's set as well... *sigh* Just waiting for Ms. Rappa to take me home.

t_ch: 现在应该是排练时间吧？
t_en: Isn't it practice time right now?

t_ch: 嘘——我是偷偷溜出来的，借口说房间里太闷，想喝瓶苏乐达，就闲逛到这来了。
t_en: Shhh... I slipped out saying that the room was too stuffy as an excuse. Then I wanted to drink some SoulGlad, and that's how I ended up here.

t_ch: 别担心，虽说乐队只是个社团活动，来去自由，但我契波大人是最讲信用的财富学院学生，答应了你们，就会认真完成演出的！
t_en: Don't worry. The band may just be a club event, and we're free to come and go as we please, but I am the most trustworthy among all Fortune Academy students. Once I've made a promise, you bet I'll be sure to complete the performance!

t_ch: 我猜…
t_en: I'm guessing...

t_ch: 要选哪个？
t_en: Which one will you pick?

t_ch: 正当你还在犹豫不决的时候，视角的余光中，你似乎瞥见队列最前端的那个苏乐达瓶…朝这边晃动了下瓶盖。
t_en: Just when you are on the fence, from the periphery of your vision, you seem to glimpse that SoulGlad bottle at the very front of the row... It just shook its bottle cap in your direction.

t_ch: 稍等，我先研究下这几个汽水瓶。
t_en: Hold on. I want to scrutinize these soda bottles first.

t_ch: 没问题，你是乐队经理，你说了算！
t_en: No problem. You're the band manager. Just say the word!

t_ch: 我记得你，就在海选的正赛现场。我们约好要在顶峰相见，最终却未能实现。
t_en: I remember you, at the qualifiers during the official competition. We promised to meet at the top, but none of it came true.

t_ch: 如今你是盛会巨星，我们仍旧无人问津。还好我很年轻，有颗不愿放弃的心。
t_en: Now you are a festive superstar, and we are still nobodies. Good thing I'm still young with a heart that never gives up.

t_ch: 现在，我们要向你发起挑战！苏乐达海选的复活赛，正式开始——
t_en: Now, we are issuing you a challenge! The SoulGlad auditions' repechage round has officially started!

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 我允许你挑战我了吗？
t_en: Did I agree to your challenge?

t_ch: 咕。可恶，我们和你不是宿敌吗！
t_en: Ugh. Blast, aren't we archrivals!?

t_ch: 我们辛辛苦苦从入围赛打上来，却在第一关被你的得分碾压，我们没有气馁，从败者组突围出来。然后，再次被你在第二关淘汰……
t_en: We worked so hard to climb our way up the ranks from the qualifiers, but were mercilessly crushed by you in the first round. Yet we didn't give up, and emerged victorious from the loser's group. And then we got eliminated by you in the second stage...

t_ch: 但这次不一样了！我们完成了队伍的补强，我们——回来了！
t_en: But it's different this time! We've improved on our team, and we're back!

t_ch: 但我真的不记得你。
t_en: But I really don't remember you.

t_ch: 就这还好意思说是我的宿敌？
t_en: Are you not at all embarrassed to say you're my archrival?

t_ch: 舞台竞技，实力说话。我们在最近的训练赛中可是无人能敌，轻取所有对手，观众都说我们是见过的*最强天团*。
t_en: Ability speaks for itself when competing on the stage. We were undefeated in the recent training matches, easily beating all our opponents. Even the audience says we're the "strongest group" they've seen.

t_ch: 想证明你很强，就在这场竞赛中堂堂正正击败我们吧！
t_en: If you want to prove you're stronger, defeat us fair and square in this competition!

t_ch: 快点吧，我很忙的。
t_en: Hurry up. I'm very busy.

t_ch: 咕，怎么可能！我们练习了这么长时间的节奏舞步，你居然能够轻易跟上。
t_en: Ugh, how's that possible! We spent endless hours practicing our moves, but you were able to keep up so easily.

t_ch: 这就是天赋的差距吗…也许，我们就是无法成功？
t_en: Is this the gap in talent... Perhaps, we were never meant to succeed?

t_ch: （嘲讽它）
t_en: (Taunt it)

t_ch: 再多说点，我已经好久没听到训练赛无敌的故事了。
t_en: Keep talking. I haven't heard the story of being undefeated in the training matches for a long time.

t_ch: （安慰它）
t_en: (Comfort it)

t_ch: 别太灰心，毕竟折纸大学是我的主场，就当是专门给我定制的胜利吧。
t_en: Don't be too discouraged. Paperfold University is my home ground after all — my win is probably inevitable.

t_ch: 哼，等着！就算这次失败了，还有下次！苏乐达天团永远不认输！
t_en: Hmph, hold it! Even if we lost this time, there's still next time! The SoulGlad Idol Group will never admit defeat!

t_ch: 接受苏乐达天团永不言败的挑战吧！
t_en: Accept the SoulGlad Idol Group's never-say-die challenge!

t_ch: （接受挑战）
t_en: (Accept the challenge)

t_ch: 喔！喷出来了！但怎么都是相同的高度？
t_en: Ohhh! It sprayed out! But why are they all at similar heights?

t_ch: 真遗憾，所有汽水瓶都喷得同样高。没有人猜对，奖品就只能继续留在我手里了。
t_en: How unfortunate. All the bottles are spraying at identical heights. No one got it right, and the prize regrettably remains in my hands.

t_ch: 都说那种过气的小猴玩偶，根本不会有人想要啦！
t_en: I told you no one wants such last-season merch!

t_ch: 哎，没劲。闲逛得差不多，该回去继续排练了。
t_en: Bah, no fun. We've spent enough time outside, and it's time to return to practice.

t_ch: 话说…刚才经理你没有下注，反而跑去跟那个广告牌聊了挺久，是不是早就预见到了这个结果啊。有什么经验诀窍，分享下呗。
t_en: Say... Manager, you didn't make a bet just now, but you talked to that billboard for a hot minute. Did you already predict such an outcome? How about sharing some of your tips and tricks?

t_ch: （吹嘘自己的社交能力）
t_en: (Brag about your socializing skills)

t_ch: 想不到吧，我和那些汽水瓶在盛会海选上有点交情，愿意给我这个面子。
t_en: Unexpected, right? I made some connections with the soda bottles during the Festivity Auditions, and they were willing to do me this favor.

t_ch: （如实讲述刚才的经历）
t_en: (Recount the recent experience honestly)

t_ch: 说实话吧，我和那些汽水瓶进行了场比赛，这就是获胜的奖励。
t_en: To be honest, I competed against the soda bottles, and this is my reward for winning.

t_ch: （装作高深莫测的模样）
t_en: (Put on a mysterious demeanor)

t_ch: 只能说，懂的都懂，不懂的解释也没用，反正你知道我有办法就行了。
t_en: The only thing I can say is, "if you know, you know." There's no point explaining if you don't. Just know that I have a way.

t_ch: 厉害！不愧是我们的经理！选择跟你果然没错，以后要带我见识更好玩的事啊！
t_en: Awesome! No wonder you're our manager! I was right to follow you. Do bring us along to experience more things in the future!

t_ch: 我先走啦，再不回去就要被乱破小姐训了。再见再见。
t_en: I'm gonna make a move. If I don't head back soon, Miss Rappa will start lecturing. Bye bye!

t_ch: 经理！…乱破忍首嘱咐我多跟着您到处走走，所以…收集灵感的时候，能带我一起吗？
t_en: Manager! Head Ninja Rappa said I should follow you around more, so... could you please take me with you when you're collecting inspiration?

t_ch: 虽然不知道…究竟是有什么用意。
t_en: I don't really know... what's the point.

t_ch: 想让你增长见识？
t_en: Want to broaden your horizons?

t_ch: 先看看再说吧。
t_en: Let's watch and see.

t_ch: 嗯。那就…麻烦您了。
t_en: Okay. Then... I'll be troubling you.

t_ch: 我还从来没来过这种…像是培育舱的地方。这里…会有经理您想寻找的灵感吗？
t_en: I've never been to a place like this... it's like an incubator. Will you find the inspiration you're looking for in a place like this, Manager?

t_ch: 咦咦，那边的毛绒玩具好像在动！
t_en: Hey, hey, that stuffed toy over there seems to be moving!

t_ch: 呜呜呜。这里…已经不需要我了喵。
t_en: Boohoo. This place... doesn't need me anymore, meow.

t_ch: 明明是你自己的问题！和声都唱不整齐，还总是乱动，队形全散了！
t_en: It's clearly a you-problem! The harmonies are not in sync, and as you're constantly moving around, the formation is completely messed up!

t_ch: 再这么拖拉下去，害我们赶不上选拔时间就是你的错了哟。
t_en: If we keep dragging things out, it'll be your fault if we miss the selection time.

t_ch: …虽然听不懂它们在说什么，但那只毛绒玩具好像被同伴嫌弃了，有点可怜。
t_en: ...I don't understand what they are saying, but that stuffed toy looks like it's being rejected by its companion. I feel pity for it.

t_ch: 你怎么啦？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 这种事很常见的。
t_en: This is quite common.

t_ch: 呜…都怪我喵。最近空间站的造物们想为阮•梅女士合唱一首赞歌，所以在进行发声训练，可是我…怎么都唱不好。
t_en: Boo... it's all my fault, meow. Recently, the creations on the space station want to sing a hymn of praise for Madam Ruan Mei, so we are going through vocal training. But I... just can't seem to sing well.

t_ch: 合唱赞歌，要的就是整齐、响亮、有气势！
t_en: For a choral hymn, what's required is unison, loudness, and impact!

t_ch: 哎，就别在这家伙身上费劲了，它在选拔时候肯定要被淘汰掉的。不如现在踢掉它，我们好好练习就行了。
t_en: *sigh* Stop wasting time on this guy. It'll definitely be disqualified at the selection phase. Why don't we just kick it out and practice on our own?

t_ch: 造物本身就是不平等的，就算再怎么努力，废物就是废物，没办法呢。
t_en: Not all creations are equal, no matter how hard one may work. Trash is still trash. Nothing we can do about it.

t_ch: 呜呜…我也想向阮•梅女士表达我对她的爱意。帮帮我吧，{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}！
t_en: Boohoo... I want to express my love to Madam Ruan Mei too. Please help me, {F#big sis}{M#big bro}!

t_ch: 我来帮你！
t_en: Let me help you!

t_ch: 哈？！唱不好是你自己的事，和别人有什么关系。
t_en: Huh!? It's your problem if you're bad at singing. Nothing to do with anyone else.

t_ch: 经理…您是能和这些小动物沟通吗？可以的话，请帮帮这个受排挤的孩子吧。
t_en: Manager... Can you communicate with these animals? If you can, please help that ostracized child.

t_ch: 看到它这个样子，不由得…就会想到自己。
t_en: Just looking at it, I can't help but... think of myself.

t_ch: 嗯，我试试。
t_en: Okay, let me try.

t_ch: 和我有什么关系？
t_en: What's it got to do with me?

t_ch: 就是就是！唱不好是你自己的事，和别人有什么关系。
t_en: Exactly! It's your own problem if you can't sing, not anyone else's.

t_ch: 经理…您是能和这些小动物沟通吗？可以的话，请帮帮这个受排挤的孩子吧。
t_en: Manager... Can you communicate with these animals? If you can, please help that ostracized child.

t_ch: 看到它这个样子，不由得…就会想到自己。
t_en: Just looking at it, I can't help but... think of myself.

t_ch: …好吧，我试试。
t_en: ...Okay, I'll give it a try.

t_ch: 谢谢喵！我总算学会该怎么合唱了，以后肯定没问题的！
t_en: Thank you, meow! I've finally learned how to sing in a chorus. It won't be a problem later on!

t_ch: 选拔就要开始了，做好准备。
t_en: The selection is about to start. Get ready.

t_ch: 哎，都说了，我们在被制造出来的时候就有差距，改不了的啦。
t_en: *sigh* As I've said, there was already a difference in talent when we were first created. It can't be changed.

t_ch: 不多时，所有的「阮•梅」造物都挤在舱段中间，依次进行了发声表演。
t_en: Not long after, all of Ruan Mei's creations are all huddled in the middle of the cabin, taking turns to perform their vocals.

t_ch: 咳咳。现在我来公布选拔结果啊。
t_en: Ahem. I'll be announcing the selection results now.

t_ch: 经过对所有成员的全面考察，结合赞歌表演的实际需要，通过名单如下——
t_en: After comprehensive evaluation of all members, combined with the practical needs of the hymn performance, the roster is as follows...

t_ch: …生气的「阮•梅」造物。
t_en: ...Ruan Mei's angry creation.

t_ch: 噢！
t_en: Yes!

t_ch: …悲伤的「阮•梅」造物。
t_en: ...Ruan Mei's sad creation.

t_ch: 不对！不公平！这家伙靠别人帮忙才能合格，根本不是它的真实水平！
t_en: That's not right! Unfair! This fellow had to rely on another one's help to qualify. That's not its true capability at all!

t_ch: 咳咳。但选拔确实是由它独立完成的，我只认可这次结果。
t_en: Ahem. But it did indeed finish the selection by itself, and I have no choice but to acknowledge the outcome.

t_ch: 喵！我好高兴喵！
t_en: Meow! I'm super happy!

t_ch: 呜呜…哇哇哇哇哇哇！
t_en: Boohoo... Waaahhhh!

t_ch: 太好了。看起来那只小动物被同伴接纳了，我们应该…是做了件好事吧？
t_en: That's fantastic. Looks like this little buddy has been accepted by its companions. We probably... have done a good thing, haven't we?

t_ch: 你详细向达凡绮讲述了刚才的见闻，包括那只造物是如何挤占了其余造物的名额。
t_en: You detail what you saw just now to Dafanchy, including the incident about how that creation wrested a slot from the other creations.

t_ch: 资质平平的人，靠着外界帮助，抢走原本属于别人的东西，经理觉得这样算是公平吗？
t_en: Someone of average talent, relying on external help, and robbing something that originally belonged to others. Manager, you think this is fair?

t_ch: 这很公平。
t_en: It is, very.

t_ch: 结果至上嘛，再说它也是靠自己的实力通过选拔，没什么好质疑的。
t_en: Results are results. Moreover, it also demonstrated its own ability to pass the selection. There's nothing suspicious about it.

t_ch: …嗯，确实。只要努力提升自己的水平，就不用害怕任何议论。
t_en: ...Yes, that's true. As long as you work hard to improve yourself, there's no need to fear any gossip.

t_ch: 这不公平。
t_en: It's not fair.

t_ch: 有点难说，但本来就没有什么是绝对公平的，只能说对方的运气不好吧。
t_en: It's hard to say, but it wasn't objectively fair to begin with. All I can say is that it lucked out.

t_ch: 是吗…不过都说运气也是实力的一部分。我运气比较好，认识了你们…嗯。
t_en: Really... Though I guess being lucky is a skill too. My luck's pretty good to have known you all... Yes.

t_ch: 想那么多干嘛！
t_en: Why are you overthinking this!

t_ch: 把想要的东西拿到手就好，纠结这种事没什么意义。别想太多了。
t_en: All that matters is getting what you want. There's no point to hesitation. Stop thinking so much.

t_ch: 也对…光是在这里烦恼，没法改变任何事，还是要积极行动才行。
t_en: That's true... Getting all indecisive over this won't change anything. Only positive action will move things forward.

t_ch: 明白了…既然乱破忍首和经理都愿意相信我，我…不会辜负你们的期待的。
t_en: Got it... Since Head Ninja Rappa and the manager believe in me, I... won't let you all down.

t_ch: 来听我的演唱吗喵？
t_en: Are you here to listen to my performance, meow?

t_ch: 记录猫猫糕的演唱
t_en: Record cake cat's performance
...*sigh*

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}，请问…你在寻找作曲灵感的时候，能不能带上我？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister} Manager, when you're looking for inspiration for your music... can you please bring me along?

t_ch: …怎么回事？
t_en: ...What's going on?

t_ch: 等等，你不是打算退出乐队吗？
t_en: Wait, didn't you decide to quit the band?

t_ch: 开始回旋了。
t_en: What's with the change of heart?

t_ch: 当然可以，就是你变得有点陌生。
t_en: Of course, it's just that you became a bit unfamiliar.

t_ch: 记得多穿点。
t_en: Remember to wear more clothes.

t_ch: 行，不过那个地方很冷，你多带点衣服吧。
t_en: Sure, but it's cold there, so bring extra clothes.

t_ch: 乱破同学反复向我强调，和你共同行动会有收获。如果是考察音乐的话，也不是不行。
t_en: Rappa has constantly emphasized to me that working together with you will be rewarding. If it's for assessing music, it should be okay.

t_ch: 别浪费时间了，快出发吧。
t_en: Let's not waste any more time and go.

t_ch: 你们好。
t_en: Hello.

t_ch: 姐姐和我说过乐队的事了，上次演出的直播我也有看，非常精彩。
t_en: Big Sis told me about the band. I also saw the last show's stream, and it was really good.

t_ch: 另类，不羁，充满激情，活力四射，像极了我年轻时候的样子啊。我喜欢你们的演奏！
t_en: Unique, unconventional, full of passion, bursting with energy, a spitting image of my younger days. I liked your performance plenty!

t_ch: …谢谢。
t_en: ...Thank you.

t_ch: 互相介绍下吧。
t_en: Let's introduce ourselves.

t_ch: 嗯…我是玲可，「机械热潮」乐队的键盘手…也是雪原科考队的队员。
t_en: Mm... I'm Lynx, the Mechanical Fever's keyboardist... and a member of the Belobog Snow Plains Research Team.

t_ch: 我叫邓恩，也是「机械热潮」的——前键盘手！现在已经退出了，哈哈。
t_en: I am Dunn, also the Mechanical Fever's... former keyboardist! I've left the band, haha.

t_ch: 艾尔菲。折纸大学谐乐学院的学生，目前…在经理的乐队里担任吉他手。
t_en: Arfey. A student from Paperfold University's Charmony Academy, currently... a guitarist in the manager's band.

t_ch: 好啊！既然是喜欢音乐的朋友，欢迎随时来贝洛伯格交流，音乐无边界！
t_en: All right! Since we're all music-loving friends here, you're more than welcome to come to Belobog for a chat anytime. Music has no boundaries!

t_ch: 你不用在铁卫禁区执勤么？
t_en: Aren't you on duty at the Silvermane Guard Restricted Zone?

t_ch: …咳。这片雪原虽然看起来没什么危险，但也还是要经常巡视，防患于未然。正好今天执勤的铁卫生病了，我就来临时顶个班。对，是这样。
t_en: ...Ahem. These snow plains may look quite safe, but they still need to be patrolled, in case of any mishaps. The on-duty guard was sick today, so I'm here to fill in temporarily. Yeah, that's the gist of it.

t_ch: 明明刚才还在和我一起烤洞螈。
t_en: You were just roasting olms with me just now.

t_ch: 这种理由，我在向学院请假的时候用过好多次了。
t_en: I've used an excuse like this so many times at the academy.

t_ch: 有发生什么好玩的事吗？
t_en: Anything fun happened?

t_ch: 听姐姐说，你们是想要寻找灵感吧。
t_en: Big Sis said you were looking for inspiration.

t_ch: 我的经验…在没什么想法的时候，就会坐在雪地里发呆。
t_en: From my experience... when I was stuck, I'd sit in the snow and zone out.

t_ch: 光是听篝火发出的噼里啪啦的声音，看锅里升起的白蒙蒙的蒸汽，都觉得很有意思。
t_en: Listening to the twigs crackling in the campfire, staring at the white steam rising from the pot — even the most humdrum occurrences were interesting.

t_ch: 只是发呆就有灵感吗？我都是得在教授的讲座和经典作品的拆解分析中寻找……
t_en: Can you really get inspired just from zoning out? I have to seek inspiration from the professor's lectures and analyzing classical works...

t_ch: 听起来很厉害…我没有学过这么复杂的知识，只是喜欢演奏而已。
t_en: Sounds really amazing... I've never learned any of that profound knowledge. I just like to play music.

t_ch: 哈哈，我也就大概是在士官学校里上过选修课的程度。
t_en: Haha, I just sort of winged it from elective courses in cadet school.

t_ch: 但你们的乐队很成功。
t_en: But your band is really successful.

t_ch: 有热情才是最重要的！
t_en: Passion is the most important thing!

t_ch: 要说能带来灵感的趣事…对了，刚才过来的路上，有个白色的东西从我眼前一闪而过，还发着奇怪的光，搞不好是什么新物种！
t_en: If I had to recall something interesting that inspired me... Oh yeah, while I was on the way here, something white darted across my path. It was glowing in a strange light — might be some sort of new creature!

t_ch: 就在不远处的雪地里，有兴趣的话可以去看看。
t_en: It's not too far away. You can take a look if it piques your curiosity.

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 不知为何，白茫茫的雪原里有只扑满正在悠闲地踱步，对你的靠近没有表现出任何警惕。
t_en: For reasons unknown, there is a trotter sauntering about in the shimmering ivory snow. It does not seem alarmed as you get closer.

t_ch: 没见过的小动物！好可爱~
t_en: I've never seen such an animal before! It's so cute!

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Hmph, hmph hmph!

t_ch: 扑满反复扭动着身子，似乎很欢迎你们的到来。怎么办，要在这里玩一会吗？
t_en: The trotter wiggles and waggles about, as if welcoming your presence. Now what? Play with it for a while?

t_ch: （陪这只扑满玩会）
t_en: (Play with the trotter for a bit)

t_ch: 好呀好呀，我也想跟它玩。来~转个圈，握握手~
t_en: Yay! I want to play with it too. Spin around, and shake hands!

t_ch: （拍拍脑袋）
t_en: (Pat its head)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Hmph, hmph hmph!

t_ch: 很明显，这只扑满相当喜欢你饱含爱意的抚摸，并热情地向你示好。
t_en: It's rather obvious that this trotter loves your affectionate caress. It enthusiastically reciprocates your warmth.

t_ch: （揪揪鼻子）
t_en: (Pinch its nose)

t_ch: 哼，哼哼~
t_en: Hmph, hmph hmph!

t_ch: 好吧，看来这只扑满并不介意你触碰它的鼻尖，甚至主动朝这边靠了靠。
t_en: Okay, seems like the trotter doesn't mind you touching its nose. It even presses its nose into your hand.

t_ch: （摸摸肚子）
t_en: (Rub its belly)

t_ch: 哼…哼哼……
t_en: Hmph... hmphph...

t_ch: 这只扑满缩起了身子，对你即将实施的行为表现出强烈的抵触，还是放弃吧。
t_en: This trotter curls up, showing great resistance to what you're about to do. Best give up.

t_ch: （差不多玩够了吧）
t_en: (Pretty much done playing)

t_ch: 好可爱的小家伙~嘿嘿，要不要跟我回学校呀~
t_en: What a cute lil fella! Heehee, how about coming back to school with me...

t_ch: 艾尔菲完全沉浸在和扑满嬉戏的氛围中了……
t_en: Arfey is totally immersed in the atmosphere of playing with the trotter...

t_ch: （询问它是否懂音乐）
t_en: (Ask if it knows music)

t_ch: 就是…你好，请问你会演奏音乐吗？
t_en: Err... Hello, do you know how to play music?

t_ch: 艾尔菲用极其复杂的眼神望着你，仿佛在思考是否应该再离你远点。
t_en: Arfey stares at you with a very complicated expression, as if she is thinking about whether to back away from you.

t_ch: 好吧，我知道…就是开个玩笑，嗯。
t_en: Fine, got it... It was just a joke, yeah.

t_ch: （现在不是玩的时候）
t_en: (It's not time to play now)

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}，能帮我个忙吗？
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager, can you help me out?

t_ch: 我还是第一次见到这么有趣的动物，麻烦给它拍个照吧，我想带回去作为纪念。
t_en: It's my first time seeing such an interesting animal. Please take a picture of it — I wish to bring it back as a souvenir.

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 难道你没有手机吗？
t_en: Do you not have a phone?

t_ch: …我的手机，是家里特别定制的，只有最基础的通讯功能，没法拍照。
t_en: ...My phone was custom-made by my family. It only has the most basic communication functions, and can't take photos.

t_ch: 哎，父母希望我把全部心思都放在学院的课业上，毕业后成为著名的音乐家。
t_en: *sigh* My parents hope that I would fully focus on my school work, and become a renowned musician after I graduate.

t_ch: 随他们怎么想吧。麻烦您了。
t_en: They can think what they want. Appreciate the help.

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 好吧…
t_en: Okay...

t_ch: 哼，哼哼——
t_en: Hmph, hmphhmh...

t_ch: 咦，突然冲出来了好几只！！赶快抓拍这个瞬间！
t_en: Hey, there's a bunch of them popping up out of thin air!!! Quickly, capture this moment!

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: 拍到了好多照片~真可爱啊~
t_en: So many pictures shot! So cuuuute!

t_ch: （收集得差不多，回去跟玲可和邓恩讲讲遇见的情况吧。）
t_en: (We should be about done with our collection. Let's tell Lynx and Dunn about what we saw.)

t_ch: 噢，原来是这样。那就没什么好担心的了，最近这种裂界生物活动得很频繁啊。
t_en: Oh, so that's how it was. Nothing to worry about, then. Fragmentum creatures such as these have been pretty lively recently.

t_ch: 所以，找到想要的灵感了吗？
t_en: So did you find the inspiration you wanted?

t_ch: 找到了。
t_en: I did.

t_ch: 算是吧…
t_en: I suppose...

t_ch: 说实话，我先前从来没想过…这种毫无意义的记录，还能成为创作灵感。
t_en: Honestly, I never once thought that... such a meaningless record could be used to inspire creativity.

t_ch: 只要能让自己产生新的想法的东西，都算是灵感。
t_en: As long as it's something that can help you create new ideas, that counts as inspiration.

t_ch: 但是…如果不按标准的乐理规范来，难道不会写出很奇怪的曲子吗？
t_en: But... if we don't follow standard music theory, wouldn't we write really strange melodies?

t_ch: 唔，复杂的知识我也不是很懂。但姐姐说过，乐队最重要的还是要让观众感到开心。
t_en: Mm, I don't really know that complicated stuff. But Big Sis said that the most important thing about being in a band is that the audience is happy.

t_ch: 不止是这些！她说还要让乐队的成员，还有演奏的自己也开心！
t_en: Not just that! She also said that the band members and the one playing should be happy too!

t_ch: …你把姐姐的话记得真清楚。
t_en: ...You sure remember my sister's words well.

t_ch: 哈、哈哈…当然了！她可是我们乐队的核心啊！
t_en: Ha, haha... Of course! She's the heart of our band!

t_ch: 经理{F#小姐}{M#先生}，我们该返回学校了吧，有点事情…需要再想想。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} Manager, we should be heading back to school. I have something... I need to think about.

t_ch: 这就要走了？我的洞螈还没有烤好啊。
t_en: Leaving so suddenly? My olms aren't done roasting yet.

t_ch: （邀请他们来观看演出）
t_en: (Invite them to the performance)

t_ch: 好呀。我也想去那个庞大的梦境里探险，等会就去问姐姐的时间。
t_en: Sure. I was also thinking of exploring a vast dreamscape. I'll go ask Big Sis about the times later.

t_ch: 我应该是走不开了哈哈。再说我也只是个前任键盘手，好久没玩乐队了，这么青春的事，还是交给你们年轻人吧！
t_en: I probably can't leave, haha. And I'm just the former keyboardist — I haven't played in a band for a long time. Such youthful stuff should be left to you youngsters!

t_ch: 但我会为你们应援的！要加油啊！！
t_en: But I will cheer for you! You can do it!!!

t_ch: 嗯，加油！
t_en: Yeah, you can do it!

t_ch: 哼，哼哼！
t_en: Oink, oink oink!

t_ch: （抓拍扑满跳起的瞬间）
t_en: (Snap the moment the trotter jumps up)

t_ch: （艾尔菲打算和我一起出发…也不是不行吧。）
t_en: (Arfey intends to head out with me... Not that it's a problem.)

t_ch: ——尽管来自遥远的冰天雪地，严寒从未浇熄我对音乐的热情，如今到访未踏足过的梦境，只为寻找那位挚友的踪迹。
t_en: Though I hail from distant icy lands, the cold never extinguished my passion for rhymes. Now I visit uncharted dreamscapes, only to seek the trail of my dear friend.

t_ch: 请你立刻停止回避，别再让我们苦苦寻觅！
t_en: Please stop avoidin'. Don't make us keep searchin'!

t_ch: 大哥！唱了这么久都没有回应，我们该不会是来错地方了吧？
t_en: Big Bro! We've been singing for so long, and still no answer. Could we have the wrong place?

t_ch: 哼。这是在考验我们的诚意，不能轻易就这么放弃！
t_en: Hmph. This is a test of our sincerity. We mustn't give up so easily!

t_ch: …没看错吧，这、这是垃圾桶组合？好有意思诶。
t_en: ...Are my eyes deceiving me? Th—This is a trashcan group? How curious.

t_ch: （很热情地打招呼）
t_en: (Warmly greet them)

t_ch: 众所周知，朋友是最宝贵的财富，尤其是当你身处险境的时候。
t_en: Everyone knows that friends are wealth most precious, especially when you're in dire peril.

t_ch: 嗨——我亲爱的桶友，见到你们可太高兴了，最近过得还好吗？
t_en: Hiii! My dear trashfriends, it's a joy to see you. How have you been?

t_ch: （假装不认识它们）
t_en: (Pretend not to know them)

t_ch: 众所周知，每个人的行为都要符合身份，而你心里很清楚，无论如何，你都不想被人当成是垃圾桶的朋友。
t_en: Everyone knows that a person's actions must befit their status, and you should be well aware that you never want to be mistaken for a friend of trashcans, no matter what.

t_ch: （看我悄悄地溜走……）
t_en: (Watch me silently slink away...)

t_ch: 大哥！就在那边，我找到那位桶友了！嗨——
t_en: Big Bro! Over there, I found that trashfriend! Hiiii!

t_ch: 忘了吗？我们是共同演出的同伴，你的说唱技巧给我留下了很深的印象。
t_en: Did you forget? We're comrades who performed together, and your rapping left a deep impression on me.

t_ch: 路上经历了太多磨难，总算来到你生活的地方，别让我们白跑一趟！
t_en: We overcame so many hardships all this way, finally arriving at the place you're living in. Don't make us come all this way for nothing!

t_ch: 这不是表演乐队的经理吗，怎么还和这些垃圾桶有关系？
t_en: Isn't this the performing band's manager? How does the manager know these trashcans?

t_ch: 好吧，现在你知道全体目光向你看齐是什么样的感受了。
t_en: All right, now you know how it feels to have all eyes on you.

t_ch: 桶友哟，你们乐队的美名从贝洛伯格的广场到垃圾场，无人不知无人不晓。因此我们专程前来拜访，希望有幸得见演出的盛况。
t_en: Trashfriend, your band's grand reputation is known from the Belobog plaza all the way to the junkyard. That's why we have specially come all this way to pay a visit, hoping that we'll have the honor of watching your glorious performance.

t_ch: 顺便为你们欢呼鼓掌！
t_en: And also to cheer you on!

t_ch: 呜呜，大哥…{F#她}{M#他}就在这里，我们终于到了啊！
t_en: Waaa, Big Bro... {F#She}{M#He} is right here. We've finally made it!

t_ch: 这份炙热的友情，不远万里来为同伴加油应援的精神…太、太让人感动了！
t_en: This burning friendship, this spirit of traveling so far just to cheer a peer on... It's... It's simply too touching!

t_ch: 但其实…
t_en: But actually...

t_ch: 我谢谢你们。
t_en: Thank you so much.

t_ch: 你向贝洛伯格的垃圾桶组合讲述了最近发生的事，包括乐队究竟遭遇了怎样不公的对待。
t_en: You tell the Belobogian trashcan group about recent events, including the part where the band is treated unfairly.

t_ch: ——那我们，来得正是时候。
t_en: ...Then we have arrived at just the right time.

t_ch: 是我们该出场的时候！
t_en: It's time for us to shine!

t_ch: 大哥！让我们向那群蛮不讲理的人示威，向所有观众发出正义的召唤吧！
t_en: Big Bro! Let us demonstrate a show of force to those ruffians and send out a call to the entire crowd for justice!

t_ch: 即便灯光熄灭，被赶下舞台，即便没有人喝彩，我们的音乐也不会停歇。
t_en: Even if all the lights are extinguished, and we're booted off the stage... Even if no one cheers for us, our music will never cease.

t_ch: ——来吧！加入我们，用最大的音量，最华丽的表演，最自信的姿态，让整个世界都记住你的存在！
t_en: ...Come! Join us! Let the world remember your existence with the loudest volume, the most beautiful performance, and the most confident poise!

t_ch: （加入垃圾桶的示威…？）
t_en: (Join the trashcans' demonstration...?)

t_ch: 桶友别再犹豫，加入这段旋律！
t_en: Do not tarry, trashfriend! Join this rhythm!

t_ch: （加入垃圾桶的示威…？）
t_en: (Join the trashcans' demonstration...?)

t_ch: ——学院代表，学院代表，你们实在让人烦躁，模样装得清高，做得相当糟糕，嘴里说着尊师重道，心里想的都是圈套。可笑！
t_en: ...Academy's reps, academy's reps, I hafta say you're all a huge pain, putting on a show of utter disdain. Spitting respect and morals out on your main, but traps and tricks are all you've got in yo brain! Refrain!

t_ch: 看见我们来到，转身撒腿就跑！
t_en: We're gonna come, come, come for your domain, till all you can do is cower in shame!

t_ch: 大哥！我们唱得再大声点，让那群躲起来的胆小鬼都听见！
t_en: Big Bro! We gotta turn up our noise and let those hiding cowards hear us!

t_ch: 刚才听垃圾桶说，学院打算换掉那只表演乐队，还要处分他们，不会是真的吧？
t_en: I heard from the trashcans that the academy is intending to replace that performing band, and even discipline them. You think it's true?

t_ch: 难说啊，就财富学院代表那个凶样，做出什么更离谱的事都不稀奇。
t_en: Hard to say. Look at how fierce that Fortune Academy's rep is. I wouldn't be surprised if he does something so outrageous.

t_ch: 啊，所以…乐队解散的消息是真的吗？
t_en: Ah, so... news of the band disbanding is true?

t_ch: 真的，学校很讨厌我们乐队。
t_en: Yeah, the school hates our band.

t_ch: 乐队的表现那么好，换掉你们实在太不合理了，明明大家都很喜欢……
t_en: The band's doing so well though. It makes no sense to replace you. Everyone obviously loves you...

t_ch: 你不相信吗？
t_en: You don't believe me?

t_ch: 倒不是不信吧，只是觉得…乐队的表现那么好，换掉你们实在太不合理了……
t_en: It's not that. I just think... with the band doing so well, it makes no sense to replace you...

t_ch: 如果学校打算更换乐队，不让你们上台…我、我会为你们声援的！
t_en: If the school intends to change bands and prevent you from performing... I—I will cheer you all on!

t_ch: 我也是！我喜欢你们的音乐，还想看「{TEXTJOIN#120}」的演出！别让那些坏人得逞啊！
t_en: Me too! I love your music, and I wanna see {TEXTJOIN#120} perform! Don't let those baddies get their way!

t_ch: 支持你们！要顶住压力，坚持自己的风格！
t_en: We support you! You've got to hang in there and stick to your guns!

t_ch: 嗯。观众们的呼声，永远是给演出者最好的回应。你们——非常受欢迎啊。
t_en: Good. The audience's cheers are always the best answer to a performance. You're all very popular.

t_ch: 嗯！和我们一样受欢迎！
t_en: Yeah! Just as popular as us!

t_ch: 大哥！这就是音乐的力量啊！
t_en: Big Bro! This is the power of music!

t_ch: 喂，那边的学生！不好好准备音乐派对，都聚在这是想干嘛？！
t_en: Hey, those students over there! What're you all doing here? You should be preparing for the music party!

t_ch: 垃圾桶？哪里跑来的脏东西，这不是你们该来的地方，滚出去！
t_en: Trashcans? Where did these dirty things come from? This is not a place you should be loitering in. Get lost!

t_ch: 实在太不讲理，立刻停止言语暴力，表示你的歉意，否则别怪我们生气！
t_en: Ain't so reasonable! Insult questionable! Say sorry and be amiable! Or we're gonna make you destructible!

t_ch: 也别想逃避我们的质疑！
t_en: And don't think of running away from fury justifiable!

t_ch: 大哥！和他正面对峙，我们不会退缩！
t_en: Big Bro! We won't back down from a showdown with him!

t_ch: 就在这里，我们兄弟三桶，对阵你们师徒三人，非得要分出个高低，为音乐正名！
t_en: Right here, right now, us three brothers will face off against you three. We will settle the score and restore rightful honor to music!

t_ch: 要打架吗，算我一个！
t_en: Is it a fight? Count me in!

t_ch: 看看我们的厉害！
t_en: We'll show you what we're made of!

t_ch: 什么乱七八糟的，还想打人，倒反天罡了是吧？清洁工呢，快收拾干净！
t_en: What's all this nonsense? And trying to pick a fight? Have you lost your mind? Where's the janitor? Get rid of these riff-raff quickly!

t_ch: 就算战胜了我们，也没法改变我们的信念。因为音乐——是杀不死的！
t_en: Even if you defeat us, you won't change our beliefs. Because music cannot be killed!

t_ch: 杀不死的只会变得更强大！
t_en: What can't be killed only gets stronger!

t_ch: 大哥！我终于找到了啊，属于我们要走的道路。加把劲，挚友！
t_en: Big Bro! I've finally found it! A path for us to walk on! Give it your all, my bosom friend!

t_ch: ——加油。记得要坚持自己的梦想啊。
t_en: ...Do your best. Remember to hold on to your dreams.

t_ch: 很久以后，见到垃圾桶时，你还是会回想起和它们共同与学院代表对峙的时刻，这样最棒的时刻永远不会再有了。
t_en: Long after this, when you see a trashcan, you will still recall the time when you stood with them against the academy's representative. There will never be another moment as glorious as this.

t_ch: 看什么看，都散了！学生就要有学生的样子，别整天跟奇怪的人混在一起。
t_en: What're you lot looking at? Disperse! Students must act like it, and stop gallivanting with strange characters.

t_ch: 说的就是你！新乐队已经组好了，没你们的份！
t_en: I'm talking about you! The new band has been formed, and you're all not included!

t_ch: 发出一声不屑的*哼*。
t_en: Let out a disdainful *hmph.*

t_ch: 我不需要！
t_en: I don't need it!

t_ch: 行啊，坦然接受自己的失败也是种能力，等音乐派对结束，有你们好看！
t_en: Good, accepting your own failure is also a kind of strength. When the music party is over, you will learn what pain is!

t_en: Fortune Academy

t_ch: ——趁那群人不在，赶紧唱个痛快，表达你们的愤慨，顺便赞扬我们无畏的气概。
t_en: ...When they ain't around, we gon' spit it loud. Let your anger out, we ain't backing down. Shout out to the fearless, we wearing the crown.

t_ch: 还要阻止他们继续使坏！
t_en: Stop 'em in their tracks, we shutting 'em down!

t_ch: 大哥！我好喜欢这个地方，这就是我们新的舞台啊！
t_en: Boss! I love this place, this is our new stage!

t_ch: 去完成你的使命吧。但要记好了——音乐，无法被任何东西击败！
t_en: Go complete your destiny. But remember... Our music ain't gonna be drowned!

t_ch: ——新游戏首次测试啊，走过路过不要错过啊。
t_en: ...New game's first test! Don't miss out!

t_ch: 来试试吧，只需体验三分钟，相信你就会像我一样，爱上这款游戏！
t_en: Give it a try. Just three minutes and I'm sure you'll love this game just like I do!

t_ch: 最近测试的游戏有点多啊。
t_en: Feels like so many games have gone into beta testing recently.

t_ch: 嗨，还不是折纸大学要举办校庆，再不上线就赶不上咯。不是吹牛啊，我开发的这部作品，怎么都能拿个「最受学生喜爱的移动游戏」奖吧？
t_en: That's because it's almost time for the Paperfold University anniversary festival. They won't make it if they don't launch now. Not that I'm bragging, but this title I developed? I reckon it can probably clinch a "Most Beloved Mobile Game by Students" award.

t_ch: 我观察了下，根本没什么有竞争力的对手，同题材的游戏更是没有，应该是稳了。
t_en: I've noticed that there's no good competition at all in this category, and games with a similar theme are basically nowhere to be found. The prize is pretty much in the bag.

t_ch: 街机算移动游戏吗？
t_en: Are arcade games considered mobile games?

t_ch: 皮皮西商人向你展示了装在街机底部的滑轮，甚至还推着机器绕场地转了几圈。
t_en: The Pepeshi merchant shows you the wheels attached to the bottom of the arcade machine. He even pushes the machine around for a few rounds.

t_ch: 怎么不算呢？
t_en: Why wouldn't they be?

t_ch: 其实，有同题材的游戏…
t_en: Actually, there is a game with a similar theme...

t_ch: 你将那位智械商人的事告诉了他，还不忘极力夸赞对方的游戏究竟有多好玩。
t_en: You tell him about that Intellitron merchant and emphasize how fun the other's game was.

t_ch: …啊？除了我以外，还有人做哈努努题材的游戏？那是明晃晃的抄袭！
t_en: ...Eh? Someone else made a Hanunue game besides me? That's just blatant copying!

t_ch: 我才是原创！不信给你看时间，文件夹老早就建好了。他就是个跟风的！
t_en: Mine's the original one! Look at the timestamp for my folder if you don't believe me — the folder was created a long time ago. He's just trying to jack the trend!

t_ch: 先给我玩玩吧。
t_en: Let me play it first.

t_ch: 好嘞！看你的模样，就知道是个懂游戏的。要是有什么不足之处，欢迎批评，欢迎指正啊。要是很满意，就麻烦写个五星好评啦。
t_en: All right! You look like someone who knows games. Feel free to critique if you find anything lacking. I welcome all feedback. If you're satisfied, please write a five-star review for me.

t_ch: 准备好了吗？我要启动了——
t_en: You ready? I'm starting it up...

t_ch: …游戏体验怎么样？好玩吗？
t_en: ...How was the experience? Was it fun?

t_ch: 非常简单！
t_en: It was super easy!

t_ch: 不不，那是你的操作太强了，普通玩家可达不到这水平。真的。
t_en: That's because you were too good at maneuvering. Average players wouldn't be able to hit such a level, really.

t_ch: 就不能自动战斗吗？
t_en: Isn't there auto-battle?

t_ch: 可以有，都可以有。只要玩得开心，所有需求都可以满足哈。我绝对听劝！
t_en: Yes, there can be! We can add anything you want. As long as you have fun, all requests will be fulfilled. As a dev, I will listen!

t_ch: 看来玩家反馈还不错，总算能放心了。实不相瞒，这还是我第一次开发游戏。
t_en: Looks like there's decent player feedback. I can now feel at ease. To be honest, this is my first time making a game.

t_ch: 别看我现在这样，年轻时候，我也玩过乐队，不是那种流行的风格…真要说的话，算是电音组合吧。在舞台上噼里啪啦的，多酷！
t_en: I may look all prim and proper now, but when I was younger, I also played in a band. It wasn't anything trendy... If I had to be specific, you could call it electronic music. All the wubs and zaps on stage... so cool!

t_ch: 那时候是真把音乐当成梦想，还鼓动队友集体退学来着，幻想靠乐队能养活自己，哈哈哈…现在回想起来，就像是个很热闹的梦啊。
t_en: I really considered music my dream back then. I even encouraged my bandmates to drop out of school together, and fantasized that we can all feed ourselves off band money, hahaha... It was a very lively dream, now that I think back on it.

t_ch: 为什么放弃音乐了呢？
t_en: Why did you give up on music?

t_ch: 所以改行来做游戏了？
t_en: So you changed professions and made games instead?

t_ch: 没办法，玩乐队很辛苦的，要创作，要排练，还要到处找表演的机会，也赚不到钱…嗨，别看演出时候光鲜，平时连发胶都要省着用。
t_en: I had no choice. Being in a band was hard — You need to stay creative, and spend time practising, and network and find a chance to perform, and you still earned no money at the end of the day... *sigh* Don't be misled by the glamor of the stage. We even had to scrimp on hair gel.

t_ch: 后来有位成员选择退出，乐队就这么解散了。兜兜转转，我到了这里，做起了游戏，有了正经的收入，也再没碰过乐器了。
t_en: A member later left the band, and then it just disbanded like that. After many twists and turns, I ended up here, starting making games and earning proper revenue. Haven't touched an instrument since.

t_ch: 哎…不留神就说多了，别太在意，就当是个前乐队成员的辛酸故事吧。
t_en: *sigh* ...I said too much before I knew it. Don't mind me. Just treat it as a cautionary tale of sorrow from a former band member.

t_ch: 会觉得后悔吗？
t_en: Do you regret it?

t_ch: 所以…为了更好的生活，放弃原本的梦想，你会后悔吗？
t_en: So... do you regret it? Giving up on your original dream for a better life?

t_ch: 没什么好后悔的，我做了当时最好的选择。
t_en: There's nothing to regret. I made the best choice at that time.

t_ch: 就算从事的工作和音乐完全没关系，我也仍然有实现梦想的机会——我想要开发出全银河最优秀的音游，让更多人听到我…我们曾经演奏的旋律。
t_en: Even if my profession is completely unrelated to music, I still have a chance to fulfill my dream — I wish to make the most superb rhythm game in the cosmos, so that more people can hear my... OUR music that we once played.

t_ch: 因为…我大概永远都忘不了，和乐队朋友们共同欢笑的那段时光了吧。
t_en: Because... I probably won't ever be able to forget the times when I laughed and played with my bandmates.

t_ch: ——新游戏首次测试，走过路过不要错过。
t_en: ...First beta for a new game. Don't miss out.

t_ch: 来试试吧，不用花任何钱，就能在游戏中得到完美的体验，绝对刺激，绝对好玩！
t_en: Give it a spin. Immerse yourself in a perfect game experience at no cost. It's guaranteed high-octane, <unbreak>100%</unbreak> assured fun!

t_ch: 最近测试的游戏有点多啊。
t_en: Feels like so many games have gone into beta testing recently.

t_ch: 哎，最近折纸大学不是在办校庆嘛，吸引来了很多游客，正好上线捞笔大的。我开发的这部作品，流水肯定能稳居前列。
t_en: *sigh* Paperfold University is holding a school celebration that has attracted many visitors — the perfect opportunity to launch my game and make some bank. The revenue generated for this title I developed will definitely be enough for the top spots in the ranking.

t_ch: 根据调查，现在没有哈努兄弟题材的游戏，又是市场真空期，非常可能成为爆款。
t_en: According to research, there are no games themed around Brother Hanu right now, and there's a market vacuum. It's very likely to become a hit game.

t_ch: 排行榜里怎么找不到呢？
t_en: Why can't I find it on the ranking?

t_ch: 怎么找不到？你看…免费榜里的音游分类，再选动画题材的近期新作，按时间排序，是不是就有了。
t_en: Can't find it? Look... under the rhythm game category in the free games ranking, then choose recent new titles in the anime theme, sort by launch time, and there it is.

t_ch: 现在都流行细分赛道，谁说小众榜单的第一不是第一？
t_en: It's all about niche markets right now. Who can say that number one in a niche ranking isn't number one?

t_ch: 其实，有同题材的游戏…
t_en: Actually, there is a game with a similar theme...

t_ch: 你将那位皮皮西商人的事告诉了他，还不忘极力夸赞对方的游戏究竟有多好玩。
t_en: You tell him about the Pepeshi merchant, and remember to lavish praise on just how fun his game was.

t_ch: 啧。我直说了吧，那就是个蹭热度的。你看，我很早就在网上宣布了立项的消息，粉丝数都比他多十几倍，现在说自己是原创，谁认啊。
t_en: Tsk. I'll just be blunt then: That person's just riding the trend. I released news of this title's development on the internet a long time ago, and my fans outnumber his more than ten times over. Now he's claiming he's the original creator. Who'd even believe that?

t_ch: 不怕。我等会就发个贴，号召我的粉丝去冲他，看他还敢不敢说话。
t_en: It's fine. I'll make a post later and rally my fans to ratio him. Let's see if he dares to talk then.

t_ch: 先给我玩玩吧。
t_en: Let me play it first.

t_ch: 行，机器摆在这，想玩的话就自己开吧。有什么建议，记得填在反馈表里。
t_en: Okay, I'll leave the machine here. You can turn it on if you want to play. Don't forget to fill out the feedback form if you've got any suggestions.

t_ch: 还行吧？玩法新鲜，画质优秀，像这样高品质的游戏现在可不多见，作为我的第一部作品，算是相当厉害了。
t_en: Not bad, eh? Innovative gameplay, excellent graphics — there aren't many high-quality games like this out there anymore. It's really impressive for my first title.

t_ch: 无敌，<unbreak>10</unbreak>分。
t_en: Flawless. <unbreak>10</unbreak> points.

t_ch: 马马虎虎吧。
t_en: Eh. So-so.

t_ch: 感觉不如《小小哈努行动》。
t_en: Can't compare to Hanu's Adventure.

t_ch: 哈哈，其实我以前也不是专门做游戏的，而是乐队的键盘手，没写过程序，但作曲能力是一流。
t_en: Haha, I didn't specialize in making games before. I was a band keyboardist. I never wrote code, but my composition skills were top-notch.

t_ch: 那时候觉得有梦想是件很幸福的事，跟着朋友离开学校，组成乐队，到处辗转巡回，就为了找个能登台演出的机会。
t_en: Back then, I thought having a dream was a blessing. Dropping out of school with friends, forming a band, going on the road playing at various venues... all to seek a chance to step on the grand stage.

t_ch: …呵，回想起来还真傻。
t_en: ...Heh, I was really dumb back then.

t_ch: 为什么放弃音乐了呢？
t_en: Why did you give up on music?

t_ch: 所以改行来做游戏了？
t_en: So you changed professions and made games instead?

t_ch: 拿音乐当饭吃太难了。演出虽然很开心，但生活总还得继续。年轻时候有梦想是很酷，但随着时间推移，还是只有梦想就很蠢了。
t_en: It's too tough to try to make a living with music. I was really happy playing shows, but life had to go on. It was really cool to have dreams as a kid, but as time moved on, it seemed really stupid to cling to nothing else but dreams.

t_ch: 乐队始终入不敷出，生存压力却越来越大。最终我和朋友大吵了一架，就退出了。后来听说他们也没能坚持下去，乐队解散了，不知道现在都在做什么呢。
t_en: The band was racking up losses, and life's pressures just became greater. My friends and I finally had a huge row, and I quit the band. I later heard that they couldn't carry on anyway, and disbanded. I wonder what they're all doing now.
